title: CBIC Streamlines Port Clearances: Physical Boarding No Longer Linked to Cargo Movement
content:
# CBIC Streamlines Port Clearances: Physical Boarding No Longer Linked to Cargo Movement

Ek important update hai! Government ne port operations mein efficiency badhane ke liye ek major step announce kiya hai. The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) ne Entry Inward aur Vessel Sail-out Clearance procedures ko standardize kiya hai, jiske तहत अब Customs officers ka physical boarding cargo clearance process se de-link kar diya gaya hai. This move aims to significantly curb delays at Indian ports.

Here are the key takeaways:

*   **Circular Details:** This update comes via **Circular No. 26/2026-Customs dated May 15, 2026**.
*   **What Changed?:** Earlier, at some ports, Entry Inward and Sail-out Clearance often required the physical boarding of Customs officers, leading to avoidable operational delays.
*   **The De-linking:** CBIC ne clarify kiya hai ki Entry Inward (governed by Sections 30 and 31 of the Customs Act, 1962) aur Vessel Sail-out Clearance (governed by Sections 41 and 42) ab physical boarding par dependent nahin honge. Physical boarding itself is an independent statutory process under Section 37 of the Customs Act, 1962.
*   **New Procedure:** Clearances will now be granted promptly upon filing of requisite documents, including Sea Arrival Manifest (SAM) and Sea Departure Manifest (SDM), and completion of prescribed checks.
*   **Risk-Based Boarding:** Customs officers ka physical boarding ab vessel ki risk profiling ke basis par kiya jaayega. Field formations ko iske liye suitable mechanisms devise karne ko kaha gaya hai, considering factors like compliance behaviour, nature of voyage, and cargo type.

This move is expected to enhance the ease of doing business and improve the efficiency of EXIM trade by preventing delays in port operations.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.cbic.gov.in/)

*Published on: 19 May 2026*
